general_science.answer: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants use sunlight to convert water and carbon dioxide into glucose (food) and oxygen, mainly occurring in leaves.
Matter exists as solids, liquids, and gases; changes between these states occur via melting, freezing, vaporization, condensation, and sublimation.
At standard atmospheric pressure, water boils at 100°C and freezes at 0°C.
Cells are the basic units of life; plant cells have a cell wall and chloroplasts while animal cells do not.
Gravity is a force that attracts objects with mass toward one another; on Earth it gives objects weight and keeps planets in orbit around the Sun.
Energy is the ability to do work and appears in many forms, including kinetic (motion), potential (position), thermal, chemical, and electrical energy.
An ecosystem consists of living organisms interacting with each other and their environment; a food chain shows how energy flows from producers to consumers and decomposers.
The heart pumps blood through the circulatory system, delivering oxygen and nutrients to cells and removing waste products.
Digestion breaks down food into smaller molecules the body can absorb; it begins in the mouth and continues in the stomach and intestines.
Respiration is the exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide; in humans it occurs in the lungs, where oxygen is absorbed into the blood.
Sound is a mechanical wave produced by vibrating objects and travels through a medium (air, water, or solids); its pitch depends on frequency and loudness on amplitude.
Light travels in straight lines; reflection is the bouncing of light from surfaces, and refraction is the bending of light as it passes between materials with different densities.
Electricity is the flow of electric charge. A simple circuit needs a power source, conductor (wire), and a load (like a bulb) for current to flow.
Magnetism is a force produced by magnets or moving electric charges; opposite poles attract and like poles repel.
Matter is made of atoms which combine to form molecules; elements are substances of one atom type and compounds are made of two or more elements chemically bonded.
A mixture is a physical combination of substances that can be separated by physical means; a compound is chemically combined and requires chemical methods to separate.
Renewable energy comes from sources that are naturally replenished, such as solar, wind, hydro, and biomass; they help reduce dependence on fossil fuels.
SI units are standard units for measurement: meter (m) for length, kilogram (kg) for mass, and second (s) for time.
The water cycle describes the continuous movement of water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection.
The SI unit of force is the newton (N). One newton is the force needed to accelerate 1 kilogram of mass by 1 meter per second squared.

indian_gk.answer: The capital of India is New Delhi.
The national animal of India is the Royal Bengal Tiger.
The national bird of India is the Indian Peacock.
The national flower of India is the Lotus.
India's national anthem is 'Jana Gana Mana'.
The national song of India is 'Vande Mataram'.
India celebrates Independence Day on 15 August each year (1947).
Republic Day is observed on 26 January each year to mark the adoption of the Constitution of India in 1950.
Jawaharlal Nehru served as India's first Prime Minister after independence.
Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of independent India.
The currency of India is the Indian Rupee, symbol ₹.
Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area.
Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state in India.
The Ganga (Ganges) is one of the longest and most important rivers in India.
Kangchenjunga is the highest mountain peak located (partly) in India, with an elevation of 8,586 m.
The mango is widely regarded and commonly referred to as the national fruit of India.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is India's central banking institution which manages currency and monetary policy.
India's Parliament has two houses: the Lok Sabha (House of the People) and the Rajya Sabha (Council of States).
The Taj Mahal, located in Agra, is a famous UNESCO World Heritage monument built by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan.
The Prime Minister is the head of the government in India. (Check current sources for the name as it can change.)
The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into effect on 26 January 1950.
India holds general (parliamentary) elections typically every five years to elect members of the Lok Sabha.
India recognizes Hindi in Devanagari script as the official language of the central government along with English for official purposes; the constitution lists 22 scheduled languages.
India's national emblem is adapted from the Lion Capital of Ashoka (Sarnath) and features four Asiatic lions.
